organizeReports.line1: Organize and structure the following {{type}} data
organizeReports.line3: Start with a one-paragraph executive summary of the key findings
organizeReports.line4: Organize findings by category with clear section headers
organizeReports.line5: Include charts or table recommendations for visual data
organizeReports.line6: Highlight anomalies, trends, and notable patterns
organizeReports.line7: End each section with specific recommendations based on the data
organizeReports.line8: Add an appendix with raw data tables for reference
organizeReports.line9: Add section headers and clear labels
organizeReports.line10: Include a brief executive summary at the top

organizeAccounts.line3: Categorize each transaction: income, expense, transfer, or adjustment
organizeAccounts.line4: Group expenses by category (food, transport, utilities, subscriptions, etc.)
organizeAccounts.line5: Calculate subtotals for each category and a running balance
organizeAccounts.line6: Flag any duplicate entries or suspicious transactions
organizeAccounts.line7: Provide a summary: total income, total expenses, net balance
organizeAccounts.line8: Compare with the previous period if data is available and highlight changes

organizeProducts.line3: Create a clean table with: name, SKU/ID, price, stock quantity, category
organizeProducts.line4: Flag items that are low stock (under 10 units) or out of stock
organizeProducts.line5: Calculate price ranges (min, max, average) per category
organizeProducts.line6: Identify pricing outliers that may need review
organizeProducts.line7: Suggest logical groupings or bundles based on related items
organizeProducts.line8: Add a category summary with total items and total inventory value

organizeSchedule.line2: List all events, tasks, and deadlines in {{sortOrder}} order
organizeSchedule.line4: Identify scheduling conflicts or overlapping commitments
organizeSchedule.line5: Suggest time blocks for deep work, meetings, and breaks
organizeSchedule.line6: Flag items with approaching deadlines that need immediate attention
organizeSchedule.line7: Group related tasks that could be batched together
organizeSchedule.line8: Suggest which items to delegate, defer, or drop if overloaded

organizeNotes.line2: Group notes by {{groupBy}} with clear headers
organizeNotes.line3: Extract all action items and tag them with owner and deadline if mentioned
organizeNotes.line4: Separate facts from opinions and decisions from discussions
organizeNotes.line5: Create a {{outputFormat}} from the raw notes
organizeNotes.line6: Highlight key decisions that were made
organizeNotes.line7: List open questions or items that need follow-up
organizeNotes.line8: Add tags or labels for easy searching later

organizeCsv.line1: Organize and clean the following {{format}} data
organizeCsv.line2: Identify column headers and data types for each column
organizeCsv.line3: Clean the data: fix inconsistent formats, handle missing values, remove duplicates
organizeCsv.line4: Standardize date formats, number formats, and text casing
organizeCsv.line6: Create a pivot table or summary view of key metrics
organizeCsv.line7: Identify data quality issues and suggest fixes
organizeCsv.line8: Output the cleaned data in a properly formatted table

organizeDatabase.line2: Analyze the current schema with a focus on {{focus}}
organizeDatabase.line3: Suggest proper table relationships and foreign keys
organizeDatabase.line4: Identify missing indexes for common query patterns
organizeDatabase.line5: Suggest data type optimizations for storage efficiency
organizeDatabase.line6: Create or improve naming conventions for tables and columns
organizeDatabase.line7: Provide SQL migration scripts for the proposed changes
organizeDatabase.line8: Generate an ER diagram description of the final schema

organizeSummaryTable.line1: Create a summary table grouped by {{group}}
organizeSummaryTable.line3: Add a total/grand total row at the bottom
organizeSummaryTable.line4: Highlight the top 3 and bottom 3 performers
organizeSummaryTable.line5: Calculate percentage of total for each group
organizeSummaryTable.line6: Add trend indicators (up/down/flat) compared to previous period if available
organizeSummaryTable.line7: Format numbers with proper separators, decimal places, and currency symbols
organizeSummaryTable.line8: Add a brief interpretation note for each significant finding

organizeTimeline.line3: List events in chronological order with exact dates
organizeTimeline.line5: Identify phases or periods and label them clearly
organizeTimeline.line6: Show dependencies: which events needed to happen before others?
organizeTimeline.line7: Highlight any gaps or periods with no activity
organizeTimeline.line8: Add a text-based visual timeline representation

organizeDashboard.line3: Identify the top 5-7 KPIs that should be displayed prominently
organizeDashboard.line4: Suggest the best chart type for each metric (line, bar, pie, gauge, etc.)
organizeDashboard.line5: Organize metrics by priority: top row = most critical, lower rows = supporting data
organizeDashboard.line6: Include comparison data: vs. previous period, vs. target, vs. industry benchmark
organizeDashboard.line7: Add status indicators (red/yellow/green) for metrics with thresholds
organizeDashboard.line8: Suggest drill-down views for each major metric
